attack_surface.data_description: Off-chain claim proof and user address

security_gap.gap_description: Weak or compromised off-chain proof generation/on-chain verification allows attackers to claim unauthorized rewards.

actor_risk.description: User attempting to claim rewards without valid entitlement.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exploiting proof vulnerabilities allows theft of incentive tokens belonging to other users.

assumptions: The off-chain service generating claim proofs can be compromised or manipulated.
The on-chain verification logic for claim proofs is flawed.

mitigation.strategy: Enhance cryptographic proof mechanisms and secure the off-chain service.

description: An attacker may forge or acquire valid claim proofs for Incentive Tokens they did not earn by exploiting weaknesses in the off-chain proof generation or on-chain verification.

attack_surface.data_description: Off-chain claim proof and user address

security_gap.gap_description: Proof format or verification is susceptible to modification without invalidation.

actor_risk.description: User attempting to alter claim details for personal gain.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Modifying proofs allows unauthorized transfer or inflated claims of rewards.

assumptions: Claim proofs generated off-chain can be modified in transit or at rest.
The on-chain verification logic does not detect modifications to claim proofs.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust cryptographic integrity checks for claim proofs.

description: An attacker could modify their legitimate claim proof or a spoofed proof to alter the claimed amount of Incentive Tokens or redirect the payout to a different address.

security_gap.gap_description: System infrastructure or contract logic is vulnerable to transaction spam, preventing legitimate participation.

actor_risk.description: Malicious user aiming to disrupt governance availability.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful DoS can disrupt critical governance processes.

assumptions: Network transaction fees are insufficient to deter a flooding attack.
The governance contract or RPC endpoint has capacity limits that can be easily reached by spam.

mitigation.strategy: Implement dynamic fee mechanisms and ensure robust network infrastructure.

description: An attacker can flood the network with transactions targeting the governance contract, preventing legitimate `$BGT` holders from submitting proposals or votes within required timelines.

security_gap.gap_description: Vulnerability in contract logic allows bypassing `$BGT` stake/delegation or threshold checks for governance actions.

actor_risk.description: Malicious user attempting to gain unauthorized governance rights.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Bypassing controls allows attacker to influence governance without proper stake.

assumptions: The governance smart contract contains a logic bug allowing threshold checks to be bypassed.
The contract incorrectly verifies `$BGT` ownership or delegation.

mitigation.strategy: Conduct thorough smart contract audits and formal verification.

description: An attacker could exploit a smart contract vulnerability in the governance module to propose or vote on proposals without meeting the required `$BGT` stake or delegation thresholds.
